The 22-item SNOT-22 is routinely included in clinical guidelines and treatment plans for chronic rhinosinusitis due to its established validation in measuring health-related quality of life. It is consistently used in both pre- and post-treatment assessments, reflecting its accepted place within standardized care routines.

Thus, yes—the SNOT-22 is a widely used measure for HRQOL in chronic rhinosinusitis because it is systematically applied in clinical decision-making and longitudinal tracking of symptoms.

Its systematic incorporation into baseline and follow-up evaluations demonstrates wide practical usage and ongoing reliance among clinicians managing CRS cases.
